title: Warm up
content: Three illustrated images show taxi drivers' perspectives from inside vehicles in Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou. Each scene reflects a distinct urban environment and driving experience unique to that city.
key_claims: Beijing scene shows a modern highway with skyscrapers and traditional architecture, driver wearing smart glasses
Chongqing scene shows a driver reading a book with mountainous, layered city architecture and a cable car visible
Hangzhou scene shows a driver using a smartphone app with a lake view and robotic/AI interface elements

title: An Evolving Nation
content: Title slide introducing the presentation theme 'A Ride Through China' with images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture representing Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou.
key_claims: Visuals include a taxi sign, the Forbidden City, a riverside city at night, and a pagoda tower

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide introduces a structured framework for investigating city development, using an isometric city illustration alongside a three-step process chain: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact.
key_claims: The research process follows three sequential steps connected by arrows

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: An interactive matching exercise pairs three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—with their respective development strategies, shown with connecting lines and supporting images of each city's architecture.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Sustainable urban development
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合)

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide connects the three city scenes from taxi drivers' perspectives to their correct development strategies, reinforcing the concept that each Chinese city tailors its growth strategy to local conditions.
key_claims: Beijing scene represents sustainable urban development
Chongqing scene represents tourism & economic growth
Hangzhou scene represents technology & tradition integration
China is an evolving nation with unique development strategies
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote development (因地制宜)
